compartment.name: Your Compartment
compartment.description:
A cozy compartment on the Midnight Express. Cold air seeps through the window frame. A narrow bed runs along the left wall, its sheets twisted from sleep. A small table sits opposite, a half-empty coffee cup still warm. The rhythmic clacking of wheels on tracks is the only sound.
Your door leads south to the corridor.

corridor.name: Train Corridor
corridor.description:
A narrow carpeted corridor running the length of the train car. An overhead light flickers, casting long shadows. The announcement speaker is mounted on the wall, silent for now. A cold draft comes from somewhere — the heating must have failed.
Your compartment door is to the north. The dining car is to the east. The conductor's office is to the west. A locked door leads south.

dining_car.name: Dining Car
dining_car.description:
The dining car is quiet this early morning. The smell of cold food hangs in the air. Half-eaten breakfast plates sit on the tables. One chair is overturned, a wine glass shattered on the floor — someone left in a hurry. A service cart stands near the waiter's station.
The corridor is to the west.

conductors_office.name: Conductor's Office
conductors_office.description:
A small office in disarray. Papers are scattered across the floor, a coffee cup overturned. The desk holds train documents, a drawer sits beneath it, and an old radio sits on a shelf — its dial still glowing faintly. Someone was here recently, and they didn't leave calmly.
The corridor is to the east.

safe_room.name: Safe Room
safe_room.description:
A small secure room at the end of the corridor. The air is cold and smells of ozone — the lock was drilled open. A large safe stands against the wall, its door hanging open.
The corridor is to the north.

strange_key.name: strange key
strange_key.description: A small brass key. It doesn't look like it belongs to you.

diamond.name: diamond
diamond.description: A brilliant-cut diamond, easily worth thousands. It catches the light beautifully.

passenger_list.name: passenger list
passenger_list.description: A typed list of all passengers aboard the Midnight Express.
passenger_list.read:
Passenger List - Midnight Express

1. Herr Mueller - luggage: 2 suitcases
2. Madame Laurent - luggage: none
3. Signor Bianchi - luggage: 1 trunk
4. Miss Whitfield - luggage: 3 bags
5. Dr. Patel - luggage: 1 case

Note: Madame Laurent checked in with no luggage at all.

manifest.name: manifest
manifest.description: The cargo manifest for tonight's run.
manifest.read:
Cargo Manifest - Midnight Express

Item: 1 diamond (3.2 carat, brilliant-cut)
Insured value: 50,000 francs
Delivery: Zurich station
Handler: Conductor Weber

Note: Item stored in safe room, level 3 clearance.

bed.name: bed
bed.description: A narrow bed, its sheets twisted and cold. Something seems to press against the mattress from underneath.

table.name: table
table.description: A small fold-down table. A half-empty coffee cup sits on it, still warm. Beside it, a crumpled newspaper from yesterday's Paris edition.

window.name: window
window.description: Darkness rushes past the window. For a moment you catch your own reflection — disheveled, wide-eyed. Then a station platform flashes by, its lights streaking past. A sign reads 'Lyons-Perrache'.

compartment_door.name: door
compartment_door.description: A sturdy wooden door with a brass handle. It's slightly ajar — you swear you closed it before sleeping.

speaker.name: announcement speaker
speaker.description: A wall-mounted announcement speaker. It crackles faintly with static, as if someone was just using it.

tables.name: tables
tables.description: Several round tables with half-eaten breakfast plates. One chair is overturned, a wine glass shattered on the floor. Breakfast was interrupted in haste.

waiter_station.name: waiter's station
waiter_station.description: A small counter with coffee pots and silverware. The waiter has not yet returned. Something is scratched into the counter with a knife point.

service_cart.name: service cart
service_cart.description: A metal service cart with shelves for dishes and silverware. A crumpled napkin clings to the underside of the bottom shelf.

desk.name: desk
desk.description: A cluttered wooden desk. The cargo manifest lies on top. Among scattered papers, a train schedule is marked up in pencil.

drawer.name: drawer
drawer.description: A locked drawer beneath the desk. It has a small keyhole.

open.name: open
open.description: The drawer is open. Inside you find a passenger list.

radio.name: radio
radio.description: An old train radio for contacting the conductor. The dial is set to frequency 147.3 — not the usual channel. The call button is still warm.

safe.name: safe
safe.description: A large metal safe. The lock has been drilled — professional tools, clean entry. The door hangs open. The scratch marks are on the inside of the door, as if someone was trying to get out. The interior shelves are empty. The diamond is gone.

start_event.name: Awakening
start_event.description:
You wake with a jolt, heart pounding. The train rocks beneath you, but something is wrong. The compartment is too cold. Your door is ajar — you swear you closed it before sleeping.

Memories surface in fragments: boarding the Midnight Express at Paris Gare de Lyon, the conductor's efficient smile, settling into your compartment. Then a sound — footsteps in the corridor, voices raised in argument near the conductor's office. Glass breaking. Then silence.

You sit up. The reading light flickers. On the floor beside the bed, something glints in the dim light — a strange key. It wasn't there when you went to sleep.

Your head throbs. How long were you unconscious?

find_key.description:
You look down at the floor beside the bed. There's a strange key lying there, catching the dim light. You pick it up.

It feels heavy in your palm — solid brass, unfamiliar. It wasn't there when you went to sleep.

announcement.description:
The speaker crackles to life, startling you. The conductor's voice cuts through the static — tense, urgent:

"Attention passengers. A diamond has been stolen from the safe room. Please remain in your compartments. I am investigating the matter. Do not leave your compartments."

The static returns, louder this time. Then silence.

open_drawer_with_key.description:
You slide the strange key into the drawer's lock. It turns with a satisfying click. Inside you find a passenger list, neatly folded.

The key fits too perfectly. This drawer was meant to be found — or someone else used this same key tonight.

waiter_clue.description:
You examine the waiter's station more closely. Scratched into the counter with a knife point, the letters uneven and hurried:

"Laurent near safe 3AM"

Someone was trying to leave a message. The handwriting is shaking — whoever wrote this was afraid.

waiter_clue_laurent.description:
The scratched message reads "Laurent near safe 3AM." You cross-reference it with the passenger list — Madame Laurent checked in with no luggage at all. If she was the one who stole the diamond, she wouldn't need suitcases to carry it out.

The pieces are coming together. The waiter saw her near the safe room, and she must have hidden the diamond somewhere on the train before fleeing.

find_diamond.description:
You kneel and peer under the service cart. Something glints in the dim light between the wheels and the floor. You reach in, your fingers closing around a small velvet pouch.

You untie the drawstring. Inside: the stolen diamond, brilliant-cut, catching what little light there is.

It must have been dropped here during the struggle. Hidden in plain sight.

call_conductor_win.description:
You pick up the radio and press the call button. Static, then a voice — tired, urgent.

"Weber here."

"I found the diamond," you say. "Hidden under the service cart in the dining car. And I think I know what happened. The safe was locked from the inside. Someone was trapped in there."

A long pause. You can hear the conductor breathing over the static.

"The waiter," he says finally. "He was in the safe room when Laurent came for the diamond. She locked him in. By the time we got the door open—" His voice breaks. "He didn't make it."

Another pause. "You've done what I couldn't, sir. The diamond is recovered. Laurent will be waiting for us at Zurich. Thank you."

The line clicks off. Outside the window, the first grey light of dawn touches the horizon. The train slows as it approaches the station.

The case is closed.

call_conductor_no_diamond.description:
You pick up the radio and press the call button. Static, then a voice — tense, distracted.

"Weber here."

"I'm investigating the theft," you say.

"I appreciate your concern, sir, but please keep your voice down. I'm handling the situation. Stay in your compartment."

The line goes dead. You can hear footsteps hurrying away down the corridor.

examine_bed.description:
The sheets are twisted and cold. You reach underneath the mattress and feel something hard — a folded piece of paper, hidden deliberately.

It reads: 'Package must reach Zurich. Meet at platform 7. — L'

The handwriting is elegant, hurried. The 'L' could mean anything — but combined with what you've heard tonight, it sends a chill through you.

examine_window.description:
For a moment you catch your own reflection in the glass — disheveled, wide-eyed. Then a station platform flashes by, its lights streaking past.

A sign reads 'Lyons-Perrache'. The train is moving fast, heading east toward Switzerland. You've been on this train for hours, but the last few hours are a blank.

Darkness returns.

examine_desk.description:
Among the scattered papers: a train schedule marked up in pencil. The 23:47 departure from Paris is circled three times.

Below it, someone has written 'Zurich — diamond delivery — Weber'.

A coffee stain mars the corner of the page. The handwriting matches the nameplate on the desk drawer: Conductor Weber.

examine_safe.description:
The lock has been drilled — professional tools, clean entry. But something catches your eye: the scratch marks are on the inside of the door.

Deep gouges in the metal, as if someone was trying to get out. Or keep someone out.

The interior shelves are empty. Whatever was in here is gone.

examine_radio.description:
The dial is set to frequency 147.3 — not the usual train channel. The call button is still warm to the touch, as if pressed recently.

A handwritten note taped to the side reads: 'Emergency only — notify Zurich control'.

Someone was using this radio not long ago.

examine_tables.description:
One chair is overturned, its legs scraped raw. A wine glass lies shattered on the floor, dark liquid spreading across the tiles like blood.

On the nearest table, a napkin has been crumpled and discarded. Someone was writing something, then stopped abruptly — interrupted by violence or fear.

The half-eaten breakfast plates have gone cold hours ago.

examine_service_cart.description:
Among the dishes and silverware: a crumpled napkin caught under the bottom shelf. You pull it free.

Handwritten in hurried script: 'L. was here. She took it. God help me.'

The handwriting is shaking. Whoever wrote this was terrified.

win_event.description: You managed to recover the stolen diamond and uncover the truth behind the theft!
